description: §19.4.3 / §19.6.6 — the boundary-ABSENT contrast. A `!`-call in markup with NO enclosing `<errorBoundary>` (and no match, `?`, or `!{}`) is unhandled → E-ERROR-002. This is the negative space that motivates `<errorBoundary>`: §19.6.6 states that inside a boundary the same call does NOT fire E-ERROR-002 because the boundary satisfies the handling requirement — so the ONLY structural difference between this fire case and the sibling clean `fallback` case is the presence of the boundary. E-ERROR-005 MUST NOT fire (there is no boundary whose coverage could be incomplete).
rationale: §19.4.3: the caller of a `!` function SHALL handle the result via match, `?`, `!{}`, or `<errorBoundary>`; an unhandled `!`-call SHALL be E-ERROR-002. With no boundary present the markup interpolation `${loadItem(...)}` handles the result in none of the four ways → E-ERROR-002. E-ERROR-005 is boundary-scoped and cannot fire without a boundary.
